features.aiInsights.faq.heading: [TODO] Common AI Insights questions
features.aiInsights.faq.q1.question: [TODO] Can I edit AI-generated questions?
features.aiInsights.faq.q1.answer: [TODO] Yes. Drafts are fully editable before the session starts and during pacing, including question text, response type, and ordering.
features.aiInsights.faq.q2.question: [TODO] Does AI Insights call third-party model providers?
features.aiInsights.faq.q2.answer: [TODO] No. Question generation and theme clustering run on Cloudflare Workers AI inside the same edge network that serves the session — there are no calls to OpenAI, Anthropic, or other third-party LLMs.

features.aiInsights.faq.q3.answer: [TODO] Yes. Theme groupings persist with the session record so recurring patterns surface across HR pulses, retros, and recurring workshops.

features.aiInsights.bottomCta.subheading: Let AI draft it. You refine it. Your team answers it.

features.privacy.faq.heading: [TODO] Common privacy questions
features.privacy.faq.q1.question: [TODO] Can we choose anonymity per session?
features.privacy.faq.q1.answer: [TODO] Yes. Each session uses one of three modes — full anonymity, cohort grouping, or identified responses — and the active mode is visible to participants before they respond.
features.privacy.faq.q2.question: [TODO] Where does AI inference run?
features.privacy.faq.q2.answer: [TODO] Theme detection and question generation run on Cloudflare Workers AI inside the same edge network that serves your session. Open-text responses are not routed to third-party model providers.
features.privacy.faq.q3.question: [TODO] What does the consent log contain?
features.privacy.faq.q3.answer: [TODO] Per-participant decisions with UTC timestamps, the prompt text shown, and the mode in effect. Exportable as JSON or CSV for DSAR responses and governance review.
features.privacy.bottomCta.heading: Honest responses start with trusted infrastructure.
features.privacy.bottomCta.subheading: Privacy is on by default — you never have to turn it on.
useCases.teamMeetings.seo.title: [TODO] Use case: Team meetings | Decisions with rationale
useCases.teamMeetings.seo.description: [TODO] Improve retros, planning, and decision checkpoints with consent rounds, ranked agendas, and recap exports into Slack, Notion, and Linear.
useCases.teamMeetings.headline: Meetings where everyone contributes, not just the loudest.
useCases.teamMeetings.subheadline: Replace silent nodding with structured input. Qesto makes standups, retrospectives, and decision meetings faster, more inclusive, and actually useful.
useCases.teamMeetings.imageAlt: [TODO] Team collaborating around a table in a meeting
useCases.teamMeetings.painPoints.heading: What makes team meetings frustrating
[TODO] Team members avoid conflict in the call and surface concerns once the work is already in flight.
useCases.teamMeetings.painPoints.time.de: [TODO] Unstructured discussion swallows the time you reserved for the priority decision.
[TODO] Three weeks later nobody remembers why the decision was made — only that it was.
useCases.teamMeetings.features.heading: Qesto tools for better team meetings
useCases.teamMeetings.features.pulse.des: [TODO] Open with a 30-second sentiment check so blockers surface before the agenda starts.
useCases.teamMeetings.features.ranking.d: [TODO] Rank topics live before discussion so the highest-impact decisions get the time they deserve.
useCases.teamMeetings.features.consent.d: [TODO] Validate buy-in and capture objections before closing — making post-meeting reversals visible early.
useCases.teamMeetings.features.recap.tit: [TODO] Recap export to Slack, Notion, Linear
useCases.teamMeetings.features.recap.des: [TODO] Send structured outcomes (decision, rationale, dissent) into the surface your team already works in.
useCases.teamMeetings.proof.heading: [TODO] Meeting metrics teams improve
useCases.teamMeetings.proof.metric1.note: [TODO] Illustrative target when ranked agendas and consent rounds are used each week.
useCases.teamMeetings.proof.metric2.note: [TODO] Illustrative target from teams that rotate pulse checks and anonymous input.
useCases.teamMeetings.proof.metric3.note: [TODO] Outcome and reasoning stay attached to the session record.
[TODO] We spend less time debating because dissent is visible before we close the decision — not after the work has shipped.
useCases.teamMeetings.scenarios.heading: Meeting formats that work well with Qesto
useCases.teamMeetings.scenarios.retro.de: [TODO] Collect candid input ahead of the call and discuss clustered themes instead of free-form debate.
useCases.teamMeetings.scenarios.sprint.d: [TODO] Rank backlog candidates before the planning conversation so the team debates priorities, not the list.
useCases.teamMeetings.scenarios.decision: [TODO] Run consent rounds before finalizing impactful changes — the rationale lives with the session record.
useCases.teamMeetings.related.heading: [TODO] Related pages
useCases.teamMeetings.related.link1.desc: [TODO] Use polls, rankings, and consent rounds inside one session.
useCases.teamMeetings.related.link2.desc: [TODO] Enable safer participation for sensitive team topics.
useCases.teamMeetings.related.link3.desc: [TODO] Scale team-level listening into org-wide pulse loops.
useCases.teamMeetings.faq.heading: [TODO] Common team-meeting questions
useCases.teamMeetings.faq.q1.question: [TODO] Can we use this in short standups?
useCases.teamMeetings.faq.q1.answer: [TODO] Yes. A session can be a single 30-second pulse — the workflow scales down without losing the recap.
useCases.teamMeetings.faq.q2.question: [TODO] Will it slow our agenda down?
useCases.teamMeetings.faq.q2.answer: [TODO] Usually the opposite. Teams report shorter cycles because circular debate is cut by ranked agendas and consent rounds.
useCases.teamMeetings.faq.q3.question: [TODO] Can we share outcomes after every meeting?
useCases.teamMeetings.faq.q3.answer: [TODO] Yes. Recaps export to Slack, Notion, and Linear with the decision and the rationale attached.
useCases.teamMeetings.bottomCta.heading: Turn your next meeting into a real conversation.
useCases.teamMeetings.bottomCta.subheadi: Join codes work on any device — no app install for participants.

useCases.workshops.headline: Facilitate without the sticky notes.
useCases.workshops.subheadline: Real-time structured input replaces physical props and messy whiteboards. Run ideation, prioritisation, and consent rounds from a single session link — and leave with exportable results.
useCases.workshops.imageAlt: [TODO] Workshop participants collaborating on a shared activity
useCases.workshops.painPoints.heading: What slows workshops down
useCases.workshops.painPoints.props.desc: [TODO] Sticky notes, markers, and the photo phase eat the convergent half of the workshop where decisions actually happen.
useCases.workshops.painPoints.distribute: [TODO] Hybrid puts remote in second class
[TODO] In-room participants dominate physical-first formats while remote attendees watch the back of someone's head.
useCases.workshops.painPoints.results.de: [TODO] Photo-based outputs are unsearchable, untransferable, and impossible to operationalize for the team that has to execute.
useCases.workshops.features.heading: One session link for every workshop format
useCases.workshops.features.ideas.title: [TODO] Parallel idea capture
useCases.workshops.features.ideas.desc: [TODO] Every participant contributes at the same time — no turn-taking, no facilitator bottleneck.
useCases.workshops.features.voting.title: [TODO] Live prioritization
useCases.workshops.features.voting.desc: [TODO] Use ranking to converge on the highest-value options with the math visible to the room.

useCases.workshops.features.ai.desc: [TODO] Open responses are grouped into themes by edge AI so the convergent phase starts with structure, not a wall of text.

useCases.workshops.proof.metric3.note: [TODO] Workshop outputs are export-ready for Monday execution planning.

useCases.workshops.faq.heading: [TODO] Common workshop questions
useCases.workshops.faq.q1.question: [TODO] Can we run this without physical sticky notes?
useCases.workshops.faq.q1.answer: [TODO] Yes. The flow is fully digital from divergent ideation through convergent ranking — and the outputs survive the handoff.
useCases.workshops.faq.q2.question: [TODO] Does it support hybrid facilitation?
useCases.workshops.faq.q2.answer: [TODO] Yes. In-room and remote participants share the same join code, the same question flow, and the same view.
useCases.workshops.faq.q3.question: [TODO] Can we export workshop outcomes quickly?
useCases.workshops.faq.q3.answer: [TODO] Yes. Structured outputs are available immediately and can be shared before participants leave the room.

useCases.training.seo.description: [TODO] Run formative checks, confidence scales, and reflection prompts with real-time feedback — Kirkpatrick L1 and L2 evidence inside the session.
useCases.training.headline: Check understanding while you still have the room.
useCases.training.subheadline: Run formative knowledge checks, confidence surveys, and reflection questions mid-session. Find out what participants actually understood — not what they said they understood.
useCases.training.imageAlt: [TODO] Trainer leading an interactive learning session
useCases.training.painPoints.heading: Why training assessments usually fail
useCases.training.painPoints.passive.des: [TODO] Cameras stay on and heads stay up while the actual concept lands with one third of the cohort.
useCases.training.painPoints.silence.des: [TODO] Public-question formats favour the confident learners and leave gaps invisible until the assessment.
useCases.training.painPoints.surveys.des: [TODO] Post-session surveys land after the room has emptied and the trainer has moved on to the next cohort.
useCases.training.features.heading: Qesto tools for training sessions
useCases.training.features.formative.des: [TODO] Run quick comprehension checks at module boundaries and re-teach where the data points.
useCases.training.features.confidence.de: [TODO] Measure learner self-rated readiness before progressing — surfaces quiet uncertainty without public exposure.
useCases.training.features.reflection.de: [TODO] Capture learner takeaways and unresolved questions in real time, scoped to the cohort.
useCases.training.features.ai.title: [TODO] AI summary support
useCases.training.features.ai.desc: [TODO] Edge-AI clusters open reflections so trainers see the misconception pattern without manual coding.

useCases.training.proof.metric1.label: [TODO] Comprehension visibility
useCases.training.proof.metric1.note: [TODO] Trainers spot gaps before the session closes — Kirkpatrick L2 inside the room.
useCases.training.proof.metric2.label: [TODO] Instructional adjustment lag
useCases.training.proof.metric2.note: [TODO] Confidence checks surface misunderstanding early enough to re-teach before the next module.
useCases.training.proof.metric3.label: [TODO] L&D evidence
useCases.training.proof.metric3.note: [TODO] Structured outputs feed reporting at L1 (reaction) and L2 (learning).
useCases.training.proof.testimonial.quot: [TODO] We can detect misunderstanding mid-session and re-teach the same hour — the smile sheet becomes confirmatory, not corrective.

useCases.training.scenarios.midModule.de: [TODO] Validate comprehension before introducing more advanced material — re-teach where the data shows it.
useCases.training.scenarios.reflection.d: [TODO] Capture takeaways and unresolved questions before learners log off — and feed the next session brief.
useCases.training.scenarios.baseline.des: [TODO] Pre/post comparisons across an onboarding cohort show confidence and knowledge shifts over the program.

useCases.training.faq.heading: [TODO] Common training questions
useCases.training.faq.q1.question: [TODO] Can learners join without creating accounts?
useCases.training.faq.q1.answer: [TODO] Yes. Learners join with a session code in the browser — no install, no account, no waiting room.
useCases.training.faq.q2.question: [TODO] Can trainers run checks during modules?
useCases.training.faq.q2.answer: [TODO] Yes. Trainers control timing — reveal, pause, and close each check based on the room's pace.
useCases.training.faq.q3.question: [TODO] Can we use outcomes in L&D reporting?
useCases.training.faq.q3.answer: [TODO] Yes. Structured outputs feed Kirkpatrick L1 (reaction) and L2 (learning) summaries for program review.
useCases.training.bottomCta.heading: Train smarter — check understanding while it matters.
useCases.training.bottomCta.subheading: No app required. Participants join by code on any device.
